titel: AMS and unemployment benefit: common questions in plain language
hinweis: Demo and prototype. Machine-assisted translation, not yet reviewed. Orientation only, not a substitute for advice from the AMS. Amounts and limits change every year.

eintraege.antwort: You register with the AMS and file the application through the eAMS account. The first time, you must come in person.
formulierungen: How do I register with the AMS?
How do I apply for unemployment benefit?

eintraege.antwort: You must be available to the AMS, have worked and paid contributions long enough, and the maximum duration must not be used up yet.
formulierungen: Am I entitled to unemployment benefit?
Will I get money from the AMS at all?

eintraege.antwort: The first time: 52 weeks of work in the last 24 months. Under 25: 26 weeks in 12 months. Each further time: 28 weeks in 12 months.
formulierungen: How long do I need to have worked?
Is half a year of work enough?

eintraege.antwort: From the day you file the application. If you still receive severance in lieu of notice or a holiday compensation, the money is suspended for that time.
formulierungen: When does the first payment come?
Why am I not getting money yet?

eintraege.antwort: About 55 percent of your last net income, plus supplements for children. For an exact figure, use the calculator.
formulierungen: How much money will I get from the AMS?
How high is my unemployment benefit?

eintraege.antwort: Yes, there is a family supplement for children and for a partner without their own income.
formulierungen: Are there supplements for children?
Does the AMS pay extra for children?

eintraege.antwort: Usually 20 weeks. With longer insurance 30 weeks, from age 40 it is 39 weeks, from age 50 it is 52 weeks.
formulierungen: How long does the AMS pay?
How many weeks will I get money?

eintraege.antwort: A claim that was not used up can be resumed within 5 years, as long as you have not built up a new entitlement.
formulierungen: Do I get my old unemployment benefit back?
Does my old claim revive?

eintraege.antwort: Attend the check-in appointments, apply for jobs, be reachable and report any change immediately.
formulierungen: What are my obligations at the AMS?
What happens if I miss an appointment?

eintraege.antwort: If you quit yourself there is a block of 4 weeks. In justified cases a waiver is possible.
formulierungen: Do I get money if I quit myself?
Block after a mutual termination?

eintraege.antwort: Anyone who turns down a reasonable job or a training course without good reason loses the money for at least 6 weeks.
formulierungen: Do I have to accept every job?
Is the money cut if I say no?

eintraege.antwort: A job that suits you, is paid properly and is within a reasonable distance. At the start you are protected from much lower paid and unrelated jobs.
formulierungen: Do I have to work below my level?
What is a reasonable job?

eintraege.antwort: Since 2026, earning a small amount on the side while receiving benefit is generally no longer possible. There are only a few exceptions. Always report any work to the AMS immediately.
formulierungen: Can I earn something on the side?
Is a small job allowed?

eintraege.antwort: If undeclared work is discovered, you have to pay back at least 4 weeks of benefit.
formulierungen: What if I work without declaring it?
What is the penalty for undeclared work?

eintraege.antwort: If you receive sickness benefit, the unemployment benefit is suspended during that time.
formulierungen: Do I get money when I am on sick leave?
Unemployed and ill, what now?

eintraege.antwort: Yes. While you receive unemployment benefit or emergency assistance, you have health and accident insurance.
formulierungen: Do I have health insurance through the AMS?
Does my e-card still work?

eintraege.antwort: If you stay abroad the money is generally suspended. For a job search within the EU there are special rules that you must clarify with the AMS beforehand.
formulierungen: Can I travel while on unemployment benefit?
What if I move?

eintraege.antwort: Anyone who receives an old-age pension or meets its conditions has no claim. Anyone who has applied for a pension can get an advance until the decision.
formulierungen: Unemployed shortly before the pension?
What is a pension advance?

eintraege.antwort: Then, on application, there is emergency assistance, if you are in hardship and remain available to the AMS.
formulierungen: What comes after the unemployment benefit?
What is emergency assistance?

eintraege.antwort: About 92 to 95 percent of the base amount of the last unemployment benefit. It is granted for a limited time but can be extended again and again.
formulierungen: How high is emergency assistance?
How long is emergency assistance paid?

eintraege.antwort: Since 2018 generally no longer. However, your own income above the marginal earnings threshold can reduce the emergency assistance.
formulierungen: Does my spouse's income count?
Is the partner's income deducted?

eintraege.antwort: There is no legal entitlement to support. The AMS decides according to guidelines which grant or course fits.
formulierungen: Does the AMS pay for a course?
Do I have a right to funding?

eintraege.antwort: You can request a formal decision within 3 months and appeal it to the Federal Administrative Court.
formulierungen: My benefit is too low, what can I do?
How do I appeal against the AMS decision?

eintraege.antwort: Yes, if you received money wrongly or through false statements. The AMS can also offset it against ongoing payments.
formulierungen: Why should I pay money back?
What is a recovery claim?
